alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum computing approaches based on tree and matrix product state tensor networks can benefit near-term devices for both discriminative and generative learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for classical and quantum computing to benefit from the same theoretical and algorithmic developments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor network circuits can provide qubit-efficient schemes where the number of physical qubits required scales logarithmically with the input or output data sizes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained model exhibits noise resilience when tested.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits any explicit claim about the trained model's noise resilience, so this preprint claim does not appear in the published set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for online learning based on running averages demonstrates high true support recovery accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed online methods are equivalent to their offline counterparts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The equivalence between the proposed online methods and their offline counterparts is proved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods provide faster convergence rates compared to conventional online and offline algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments indicate that the proposed methods have a faster convergence rate compared to conventional online and offline algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Both assert faster convergence; the publication frames this as indicated by numerical experiments and assigns a lower certainty to the empirical claim, therefore the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel theoretical true support recovery and convergence guarantees are provided for some methods in the proposed framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel theoretical true support recovery and convergence guarantees are provided for some methods in the proposed framework.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep neural network-based framework significantly improves the performance of existing deep networks for person re-identification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: For a wide class of reasonable parameterized quantum circuits, the probability that the gradient along any reasonable direction is non-zero to some fixed precision is exponentially small as a function of the number of qubits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DNN architecture demonstrates the feasibility of a scalable indoor localization system based on Wi-Fi fingerprinting. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the exact estimation tasks demonstrated (building/floor and floor-level coordinates), narrowing the broad feasibility claim to concrete capabilities while keeping overall certainty high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the DNN-based indoor localization system can provide near state-of-the-art performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scalable DNN architecture can achieve near state-of-the-art performance with just a single DNN. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the detail that a single DNN suffices (narrowing/clarifying) and reports the claim with higher certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN architecture allows for lower complexity and energy consumption at mobile devices. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed system enables lower complexity and energy consumption at mobile devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same practical conclusion but reports it with reduced certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method called Blind Image Deblurring using Row-Column Sparsity (BD-RCS) effectively addresses the challenge of large motion deblurring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method called blind image deblurring using row-column sparsity (BD-RCS) effectively addresses the challenge of large motion in blind image deblurring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BD-RCS solves two new optimization problems involving row and column sparsity to automatically determine blur kernel and image support. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BD-RCS solves two new optimization problems to automatically determine blur kernel and image support sequentially. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the two optimization problems are solved sequentially, adding procedural detail without changing the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that BD-RCS yields better results than state of the art methods, particularly for large motion blur. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that BD-RCS yields better results than state of the art methods, particularly for large motion blur. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim is carried over verbatim, preserving scope and reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates a connection between demographic and psychological attributes and digital behavioural records. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates a connection between demographic and psychological attributes and digital behavioural records. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning framework predicted demographic attributes with good accuracy as measured by the weighted AUROC score. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive features identified may be useful for designing personalised services and communication strategies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A semi-handcrafted representation learning method for LiDAR point clouds using siamese LocNets is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The global localization system demonstrates high accuracy when evaluated on the KITTI dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the global localization system is compared with other algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) can help address several major challenges facing research in water sciences such as inter-disciplinarity, data discoverability, hydrologic scaling, equifinality, and needs for parameter regionalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various physical and geoscientific disciplines have utilized DL to address data challenges, improve efficiency, and gain scientific insights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) is transforming many scientific disciplines, but its adoption in hydrology is gradual.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the cross-disciplinary impact claim but adds a caveat about gradual adoption in hydrology, which revises and tempers the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL is especially suited for information extraction from image-like data and sequential data.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim matches this specific methodological suitability statement, so it appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new area termed 'AI neuroscience' has been born, where scientists interpret the decision process of deep networks and derive insights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new field of AI neuroscience opens up many opportunities for scientists to use DL as an exploratory tool for scientific advancement.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the emergence of 'AI neuroscience'; the publication frames it more as opportunities for exploration, a clearer and slightly less absolute wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL can use data to condition neurons that mimic problem-specific fundamental organizing units, thus revealing emergent behaviors of these units.
alignments.rationale: The specific mechanistic claim about conditioning neurons and emergent behaviors does not appear among the publication claims and is thus omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system achieves state-of-the-art detection performance for early detection of audio events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system achieves state-of-the-art detection performance for early detection of audio events.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The monotonicity of the detection function is required for reliable early detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The monotonicity of the detection function is required for reliable early detection.
alignments.rationale: This theoretical/required-condition claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Even partial events are sufficient to achieve good performance similar to that obtained when an entire event is observed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Even partial events are sufficient to achieve good performance similar to that obtained when an entire event is observed.
alignments.rationale: The empirical observation about partial events remains the same in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology includes a novel inference step coupled with dual parallel tailored-loss deep neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a methodological detail about the architecture/inference that is not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Information Multi-Bottlenecks (IMBs) are proposed as an extension of Information Bottleneck to better fit the context of neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The maximum likelihood estimate (MLE) principle in the context of stochastic neural networks (SNNs) becomes a special case of the variational Markov information bottleneck (MIB).
alignments.rationale: The publication formalizes and renames the proposed multi-bottleneck idea as a variational/Markov MIB and relates it to MLE; this clarifies and formalizes the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multiple optimality of IMB is not simultaneously achievable for stochastic encoders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Markov bottlenecks cannot simultaneously achieve their information optimality in a non-collapse MIB.
alignments.rationale: The published statement restates the same theoretical limitation about simultaneous information optimality for the Markov/IMB setting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A compromised scheme of IMB generalizes the maximum likelihood estimate (MLE) principle in the context of stochastic neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The maximum likelihood estimate (MLE) principle in the context of stochastic neural networks (SNNs) becomes a special case of the variational Markov information bottleneck (MIB).
alignments.rationale: Both texts express the same relationship between the proposed bottleneck scheme and MLE, so the core claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of IMB is demonstrated on classification tasks and adversarial robustness in MNIST and CIFAR10.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variational MIB empirically improves performance in classification, adversarial robustness, and multi-modal learning in MNIST.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports similar empirical improvements but alters the reported experimental scope (mentions multi-modal learning and MNIST specifics), so the empirical claim is clarified/adjusted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The variational MIB can encourage better information flow in SNNs compared to MLE.
alignments.rationale: This comparative claim about improved information flow relative to MLE appears in the publication but was not stated explicitly in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A binomial random variable $X$ with parameters $n$ and $0.29/n \le p < 1$ exceeds its expectation with probability at least $1/4$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A binomial random variable X with parameters n and 0.29/n ≤ p < 1 exceeds its expectation with probability at least 1/4.
alignments.rationale: The published claim is the same mathematical result with equivalent notation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For $1/n \le p < 1 - 1/n$, the random variable $X$ exceeds its expectation by more than one with probability at least $0.0370$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For 1/n ≤ p < 1 - 1/n, the random variable X exceeds its expectation by more than one with probability at least 0.0370.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this probabilistic bound identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both probabilities of exceeding expectation approach $1/2$ when $np$ and $n(1-p)$ tend to infinity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both probabilities of exceeding the expectation approach 1/2 as n p and n (1 - p) tend to infinity.
alignments.rationale: The asymptotic convergence claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapting the acoustic model of a DNN-based speech recognition system to an RNN language model can improve transcription accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Promising nodule detection performance can be achieved using weak labels through convolutional neural networks for radiograph classification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural networks utilize a large number of weakly-labelled images combined with a smaller number of manually annotated x-rays for training.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first architecture extracts saliency maps from high-level convolutional layers and compares the estimated position of a nodule against the ground truth.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second approach consists of a recurrent attention model that learns to observe a short sequence of smaller image portions through reinforcement learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The second approach employs a recurrent attention model that learns to focus on image portions likely to contain nodules through reinforcement learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method requires prior knowledge of a target's function derivatives of several orders for gradient-based training. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended cost function allows for 140--1000 times more accurate approximation for simple cases compared to conventional training methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for 140-1000 times more accurate approximation for simple cases compared to conventional training methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P1's quantitative accuracy claim identically in scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can solve differential equations with neural networks without requiring prior knowledge of target derivatives. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cost function can be extended by differentiating the equation itself, eliminating the need for prior information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q2 matches P2 but adds the mechanism (differentiating the equation) by which prior information can be eliminated, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the proposed cost function for solving differential equations resulted in 13 times more accurate results and could be achieved with a 3 times larger grid step. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is effective for solving differential equations with neural networks, achieving 13 times more accuracy using seven times fewer grid points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 preserves the 13x accuracy claim but modifies the numerical grid-size claim ("3 times larger grid step" vs "seven times fewer grid points"), representing a quantitative revision of the preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A GPU-efficient algorithm for calculating the gradient of the extended cost function is proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A GPU-efficient algorithm for calculating the gradient of the extended cost function is proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q4 restates P4 directly with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods demonstrate speed and performance advantages for multi-object tracking in dense small object scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image-to-trajectory framework demonstrates advantages in speed and performance for dense small object tracking.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about speed and performance for dense small-object tracking with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new one-to-many constraint is proposed to address track exclusions during complex occlusions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of one-to-many constraints via extended 0-1 programming enables effective hypothesis selection to manage track exclusions due to merged detections.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same one-to-many idea but clarifies its implementation (extended 0-1 programming) and frames it as hypothesis selection; certainty is reduced to medium in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-appearance segmentation developed for detection exploits tree-like topological information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The revised Local Contrast Method and the proposed multi-appearance variant effectively handle frequent merged detections.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts focus from describing a topology-exploiting segmentation to reporting that its multi-appearance variant effectively handles merged detections, narrowing the stated scope and presenting a stronger result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Batch optimization and advanced pruning technologies are employed to reduce the high complexity associated with dense object tracking.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about batch optimization and pruning to address complexity that was not present as a standalone claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The partial sum of the tubal nuclear norm (PSTNN) is proposed as a surrogate of the tensor tubal multi-rank.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The partial sum of the tubal nuclear norm (PSTNN) is proposed as a surrogate of the tensor tubal multi-rank.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two PSTNN-based minimization models are built for tensor completion and tensor principal component analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two PSTNN-based minimization models are built for tensor completion and tensor principal component analysis.
alignments.rationale: The models are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two algorithms based on the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) are developed to solve the proposed PSTNN-based tensor recovery models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two algorithms based on the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) are developed to solve the proposed PSTNN-based tensor recovery models.
alignments.rationale: Algorithmic contributions are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed PSTNN in tensor recovery problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed PSTNN in tensor recovery problems.
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority claim is unchanged in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weighted atom-centered symmetry functions (wACSFs) are effective descriptors for predicting chemical properties such as enthalpies or potential energies via machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weighted atom-centered symmetry functions (wACSFs) provide a more efficient descriptor for predicting chemical properties compared to conventional atom-centered symmetry functions (ACSFs).
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the descriptor comparatively (more efficient vs. ACSFs) which clarifies and sharpens the original effectiveness claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A substantially smaller number of wACSFs than ACSFs is needed to achieve comparable spatial resolution of molecular structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A substantially smaller number of wACSFs is required to achieve comparable spatial resolution of molecular structures compared to ACSFs.
alignments.rationale: The result about requiring fewer descriptors is restated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The smaller set of wACSFs leads to significantly better generalization performance in machine learning potentials compared to the larger set of conventional ACSFs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of wACSFs results in significantly better generalization performance in machine learning potentials than the use of conventional ACSFs.
alignments.rationale: The generalization-performance result is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intrinsic parameters of the wACSF descriptors can be optimized with a genetic algorithm in a highly automated manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intrinsic parameters of the wACSF descriptors can be optimized using a genetic algorithm in a highly automated manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about GA-based automated parameter optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple empirical parametrization scheme is sufficient to obtain high accuracy in HDNNPs using wACSFs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple empirical parametrization scheme is sufficient to obtain high accuracy in HDNNPs when using wACSFs.
alignments.rationale: The empirical parametrization claim is essentially identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a novel approach to generate synthetic web robot traffic with high fidelity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces a novel approach to generate synthetic web robot traffic with high fidelity.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated traffic accounts for both the temporal and behavioral qualities of robot traffic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated traffic accounts for both the temporal and behavioral qualities of robot traffic.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of temporal and behavioral qualities is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the traffic generator shows that the characteristics of generated traffic closely match those of the original data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of the traffic generator shows that the characteristics of generated traffic are comparable to those of the original data.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly softens phrasing from 'closely match' to 'comparable', clarifying and modestly tempering the strength of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated traffic affects cache performance similarly to actual traffic when using common LRU and LFU eviction policies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated traffic affects cache performance similarly to actual traffic when using common LRU and LFU eviction policies.
alignments.rationale: The cache-performance result is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Multi-Attribute Robust Component Analysis (MA-RCA) outperforms compared techniques on problems such as UV completion and age progression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MA-RCA outperforms compared techniques in UV denoising, UV completion, facial expression synthesis, and age progression.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the list of tasks where MA-RCA outperforms (adding denoising and expression synthesis), clarifying and broadening the original performance claim with strong wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MA-RCA is suitable for facial UV maps containing a considerable amount of missing information and outliers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Multi-Attribute Robust Component Analysis (MA-RCA) technique is suitable for facial UV maps with considerable missing information and outliers.
alignments.rationale: The suitability claim for MA-RCA on UV maps with missing data/outliers is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utilization of 3D face alignment introduces new challenges, particularly in the analysis of facial texture images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing 3-D face alignment improves 2-D face alignment by alleviating issues with artifacts and warping effects in texture images.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized challenges introduced by 3D alignment, while the publication frames 3D alignment as an improvement, representing a substantive revision in stance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Component analysis techniques need to be developed to alleviate issues arising from missing information and gross outliers in facial images.
alignments.rationale: The explicit call-for-development / limitation statement present in the preprint does not appear as a direct claim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The collection of large-scale three-dimensional face models has significantly advanced the field of 3-D face alignment in-the-wild.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a contextual claim about advances in 3-D face alignment from large-scale datasets that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model generalizes quantum decision theory to a multi-agent society of decision makers who interact through information exchange. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model generalizes quantum decision theory to a multi-agent society of decision makers who interact through information exchange. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim texts are identical in meaning and scope; no substantive change is evident.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicts that the probabilistic behavior of decision makers at the initial stage of the process can be described quantitatively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and assertion are the same between versions, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is proved that there is a decrease in the difference between the initial prospect probabilities and the related utility factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is proved that there is a decrease in the difference between the initial prospect probabilities and the related utility factors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted numerical values from the model are in very good agreement with empirical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted numerical values from the model are in very good agreement with empirical data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Asymmetric nonlinear weight update significantly compromises the online training capability of hardware neural networks based on analog resistive synapses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Asymmetric nonlinear weight update significantly compromises the online training capability of hardware neural networks based on analog resistive synapses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: New insights on engineering activation functions and a threshold weight update scheme effectively suppress the undesirable training noise induced by inaccurate weight update. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed evolutionary algorithm self-modifies its operators while evolving candidate solutions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach addresses convergence and lack of diversity issues in evolutionary algorithms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The operators in the proposed algorithm are represented as trees and evolved using genetic programming techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An analysis of operator evolution is provided in the study. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many biological and cognitive systems are poised at critical points located at phase transitions in their parameter space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many biological and cognitive systems are poised at critical points located at phase transitions in their parameter space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In both the Mountain Car and Acrobot scenarios, the neural controller reaches a point of criticality, coinciding with a transition point between two regimes of the agent's behaviour. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural controller in artificial embodied agents reaches a point of criticality, coinciding with a transition point between two regimes of the agent’s behaviour. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptation to criticality could be used as a general adaptive mechanism in some circumstances. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptation to criticality could be used as a general adaptive mechanism in some circumstances. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The echoing problem in retrieval-based conversation systems leads to low-quality responses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The echoing problem in retrieval-based conversation systems leads to low-quality responses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing a hard negative mining approach at the training stage mitigates the echoing problem. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting model from the hard negative mining approach achieves better results in terms of Average Precision and Recall@N metrics compared to models trained without this approach. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Quantum-behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) algorithm improves the performance of the Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Quantum-behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) algorithm improves the performance of the Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QPSO FCM provides comparable and in most cases superior results compared to PSO K-Means and QPSO K-Means. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QPSO FCM provides comparable and in most cases superior results compared to PSO K-Means and QPSO K-Means. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The global search ability of the QPSO algorithm helps in avoiding stagnation in local optima. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed mixture of probabilistic partial canonical correlation analysis (MPPCCA) extracts causal patterns from two multivariate time series. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mixture of probabilistic partial canonical correlation analysis (MPPCCA) extracts causal patterns from two multivariate time series. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The MPPCCA can estimate multiple partial canonical correlations from data series without any supervised signals to split the data as clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In synthetic data experiments, the MPPCCA estimated the multiple partial canonical correlations more accurately than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The MPPCCA is applicable to various types of signals, including brain signals, human communication, and nonlinear complex multibody systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Ant Lion Optimizer (ALO) is employed as a wrapper feature selection method. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There are interesting connections between different lines of work on the security of non-deep learning algorithms and deep learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Jet topics can also determine separate quark and gluon rapidity spectra from a mixed Z-plus-jet sample. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention filter step within the convolution layer prevents fill-in and guarantees an upper bound on computational resources. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptation of back-propagation allows for the combination of the proposed approach with standard learning frameworks while benefiting from sparsity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PTAV outperforms many deep learning based algorithms in tracking accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PTAV outperforms many deep learning based algorithms in tracking accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collaboration between the tracker T and verifier V enhances both efficiency and discriminative power. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Directly training deep neural networks with task-specific loss functions yields much faster and more stable convergence across problems and datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Directly training deep neural networks with task-specific loss functions yields faster and more stable convergence across problems and datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the convergence claim but tones down the adverbial intensifier ("much"), reflecting a slightly tempered presentation of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed algorithms converge to first order stationary points despite optimizing complex objective functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms assure convergence to first order stationary points despite optimizing complex objective functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core convergence claim is the same, but the publication reports it with lower stated certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our techniques require fewer training samples to achieve a desired level of convergence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms require fewer training samples to achieve a desired level of convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is unchanged but the publication expresses it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithms result in a substantial reduction in training time compared to traditional approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms provide a substantial reduction in training time compared to traditional approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel deep-learning method achieves a high-quality alignment of cross-spectral images with sub-pixel accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our deep-learning approach achieves accurate alignment of cross-spectral images with sub-pixel accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same technical result with equivalent precision claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach is more accurate than other existing methods in the task of VIS to NIR registration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is more accurate than contemporary state-of-the-art methods in the task of VIS to NIR registration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims the same comparative performance against state-of-the-art methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned descriptor used in our method is invariant to different spectra. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a feature-based approach for registering visible (VIS) to Near-Infra-Red (NIR) images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes a feature-based approach but does not explicitly claim that the learned descriptor is spectrum-invariant, weakening the original claim about invariance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper establishes an extension of the famous defect theorem. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper establishes an extension of the famous defect theorem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution/claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors provide a series of examples of finite complete θ-invariant codes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The authors provide a series of examples of finite complete θ-invariant codes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A formula is established that allows embedding any non-complete θ-invariant code into a complete one. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A formula is established that allows embedding any non-complete θ-invariant code into a complete one. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same formal result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the family of thin θ-invariant codes, maximality and completeness are two equivalent notions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the family of thin θ-invariant codes, maximality and completeness are two equivalent notions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this theoretical conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep reinforcement learning algorithm based on echo state network cells allows each UAV to learn its optimal path, transmission power level, and cell association vector. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep reinforcement learning algorithm based on echo state network cells allows each UAV to learn its optimal path, transmission power, and cell association vector. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical capability claim with only minor wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm reaches a subgame perfect Nash equilibrium upon convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm reaches a subgame perfect Nash equilibrium upon convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The equilibrium convergence claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed scheme achieves better wireless latency per UAV and rate per ground user while requiring a number of steps comparable to a heuristic baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed scheme achieves better wireless latency per UAV and rate per ground user while requiring a number of steps comparable to a heuristic baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance comparisons and computational-step claims are carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal altitude of the UAVs varies based on ground network density and UE data rate requirements, significantly affecting interference levels and wireless transmission delay. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal altitude of the UAVs varies based on ground network density and UE data rate requirements, significantly affecting interference levels and wireless transmission delay. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about altitude dependence and its effects is presented the same way in the publication.
